title: How to use monday.com for training your team on monday.com

posts.author: PedroM (Pedro Malaga - monday.com Certified Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone,

I’m Pedro, I am a monday.com referral partner and project manager who supports companies implementing monday.com and providing consultation and training.

I just wanted to share how I use monday.com for training monday.com with one of my boards. I created it to onboard new team members in monday.com, and I hope it may serve as inspirations for others.

The training roadmap is planned to be used by each team member as his learning path with dedicated links to the relevant topics. Some of the tasks are completed during the training sessions and the rest are performed later by the person.

General training2242×632 53.6 KB

A long text column is added to be able to take his own notes. It also incorporates an estimated required time to abord each topic.

Each topic is categorized, so depending on the needs of the users the entry level should primarily focus in the Basic level, then Advanced and finally Master.

Learn the Basics
The learning path starts with a quick introduction to the basics of monday.com and the nomenclature that will need to be interiorized.

Captura de pantalla (241)2647×426 58.2 KB

Boards, Groups and Items
This group covers the basics to start working with the projects, using groups and items, and managing the principles of collaboration.

Boards, Groups and Items2216×756 143 KB

Communicate and Collaborate
This section is focused in communication to acquire more knowledge about how notifications work, and to learn the essentials of how to collaborate efficiently with other teammates.

Communicate and Collaborate2223×622 105 KB

Organize your work
This is part of the advanced path in order to learn create filters, perform advanced searches and prioritize the own tasks.

Organize your work2214×522 81.1 KB

Become a monday.com Master
This is a starting point to become a master in monday.com, it provides a link to the main resources in other to go deeper in the preferred area.

Become a monday.com Master2213×629 98.9 KB

I hope it may serve as inspiration for onboarding your teammates in monday.com!

posts.author: Ben-ElecInn (Ben Curry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for sharing this.

Do you duplicate this and assign to each new team member?

How do you use this for multiple people.

posts.author: PedroM (Pedro Malaga - monday.com Certified Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Ben-ElecInn , you have two options:

  1. To create a dedicated board per team member. You can use a template or automate the creation of it from another board changing and status.
  2. To share a common board for all the team members and just add a new column for each new team member that you can restrict the edition or the view.
  • This one may be simpler if you just want to check they have completed the training. The creation of a new column could be also automated using Make.
    Hope it helps.

posts.author: jazminevc (Jazmin Velez)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Pedro, I’d love to learn more about your training raodmap, would you be able to provide me a link? Thanks!
